commands.notes: 33 tests passed, covering council browser utilities, insert fallback, permission store, filesystem server marker/token loop, gateway approve/reject, result enhancer parsing, manual write_file refusal detection, manual result unwrapping, and background permission hooks.
Legacy background script parses after adding webAgentManualToolCall.
Legacy content result enhancer parses after adding refusal detection and manual takeover UI.
Local gateway is running and reports permissionApproval=true.

Claude Code read-only review of the key manual takeover snippets returned NO_ISSUES_FOUND. Earlier strict full-repo review attempts timed out, and the provided resume id was not found in this CLI context.

passed: Content script detects model refusal text that mentions write_file, permission/path whitelist signals, a Windows path, and content.
Detected refusal renders a manual panel with reject and allow execution actions.
Manual allow sends webAgentManualToolCall to the background script with write_file path/content arguments.
Background manual tool call only accepts write_file and validates path/content are strings.
Manual calls still execute through Uy(Pt(), toolName, args, adapterName), so the local MCP backend remains the final write authority.
Permission markers returned by manual calls are remembered with original args and can flow into the existing approve-and-retry token path.
Content result unwrapping preserves raw WEB_AGENT_PERMISSION_REQUEST text so the existing approve/retry UI can appear after manual allow.
The aggregate scan calls enhanceAllCards and enhanceManualWriteRequests without recursion.
Local gateway health confirms permission approval support is active.

not_run.command: Full unpacked legacy extension + live MCP server + real model page E2E
not_run.reason: Automated local checks passed, but final visual confirmation requires the user to reload the unpacked extension and trigger a real model refusal in Chrome.
Requires installed extension lifecycle control in Chrome.
Requires installed extension and multiple provider tabs.

summary: The legacy plugin now has a manual takeover path for model write_file refusals. Automated tests, syntax checks, gateway health, and a bounded Claude review passed.
residual_risk: The browser UI still needs one real user-side test on a live model page after reloading the unpacked legacy extension.
